proof_stages.label: Virtual domination problem and degree-two theorem
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines domination and virtual domination, recalls standard monotonicity obstructions, states that every closed hyperbolic 3-manifold has a finite cover mapping with degree 2 to any closed 3-manifold, and poses the degree-one and nonhyperbolic-source variants.

proof_stages.label: Pinching criterion through the 0-2 handlebody
proof_stages.summary: To prove virtual domination it suffices to find in a finite cover M' a codimension-zero submanifold L with a degree-k relative map to the union N_2 of the 0-, 1-, and 2-handles of N, since the complement can be pinched and filled to extend the map to all of N.

proof_stages.label: Universal-orbifold handle model for the target
proof_stages.summary: The target N is given a controlled handle structure by realizing it as a finite cover of the Hilden-Lozano-Montesinos-Whitten universal orbifold, whose symmetric local model controls the later attaching curves.

proof_stages.label: Hyperbolic realization of zero- and one-handles
proof_stages.summary: Inside the hyperbolic source M, zero-cells are represented by points and one-cells by long based geodesics whose tangent directions and homology are controlled using mixing/frame-flow input from Liu-Markovic.

proof_stages.label: Almost totally geodesic surfaces and the Z2 obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Two-cells are represented by almost totally geodesic surfaces supplied by the Liu-Markovic good-pants theorem, and the Z2 invariant governing whether a good multicurve bounds is killed by taking two copies, explaining the degree 2.

proof_stages.label: Assembly of the immersed two-complex
proof_stages.summary: The points, long geodesics, almost totally geodesic surfaces, and connector pieces are glued into a pi1-injective immersed two-complex Z whose neighborhood maps to N_2 with degree 2 because the universal-orbifold handle structure makes the attaching data match.

proof_stages.label: LERF lifting and completion of the domination map
proof_stages.summary: Using the Wise/Agol separability theorem that hyperbolic 3-manifold groups are LERF, the pi1-injective immersed complex is lifted to an embedded complex in a finite cover, its neighborhood supplies the required L, and the pinching criterion finishes the degree-two map.

proof_stages.label: Volume-efficiency question for virtual domination
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines the domination-efficiency ratio involving simplicial volumes, notes that its infimum is at least 1 and depends only on commensurability classes, and states a uniform upper bound C_M independent of the target while leaving the sharp value open.
